name: Customer asks a question instead of accepting/declining
description: Public quote page has an 'Ask a question' option. Customer's message routes to the contractor as an inbound conversation, threaded against the quote, without exposing the contractor's email/phone publicly.

persona.name: Janet Park
persona.context: Wants to know if the paint price includes primer.

preconditions: Public quote page loaded for an undecided quote

steps.expect: Inline composer with a short text field; no required email/name (already known from the quote)

steps.action: Types 'Does this include primer?' and submits
steps.expect: Confirmation: 'Sent — Lena will get back to you'
Lena's /messages shows the inbound thread with a link to the quote; her assistant offers a quick draft reply ('Yes, primer is included in the labor line')
Customer receives the reply via email, threaded so it stays in the same Gmail conversation

evaluationCriteria.check: Question composer matches the public quote page's tone — quiet, not chatbot-y
Customer doesn't have to leave the page, sign up, or download anything to ask
Reply email's In-Reply-To header points to the original message, preserving Gmail threading
Composer label is associated; submit button is keyboard reachable
